name: Crunchy Bean Sprouts with Beef
recipeInstructions:
Preparation: Rinse bean sprouts; drain. Separate layers of onion and slice into thin strips to match the sprouts. Cut steak into slices across the grain, approximately 1/4 inch wide by 2 inches long. Mix stock, soy sauce, and sherry in a cup.
Stir-frying: Heat peanut oil in a wok over medium heat until shimmering. Add sliced flank steak and stir-fry for about 1 minute or until the meat loses its pink color. Remove the steak from the wok and set aside.
Add the onion slices to the same hot wok, stirring occasionally. Pour in chicken stock, dark soy sauce, dry sherry, and cornstarch paste, then bring the mixture to a boil. Sprinkle with Szechuan peppercorns mixed with salt. Return the steak to the wok and toss everything together.
Drain the bean sprouts under cold water for about 3 minutes, then add them to the wok along with the remaining sauce. Cook for another minute until just heated through. Serve hot over a bed of drained bean sprouts and onions.
